title: Sonam-Ha'ar
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:SonamHaar-TEC.jpg]] |name=Sonam-Ha'ar |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We will drive the Sith back to their dark empire!|Sonam-Ha'ar|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]]}} '''Sonam-Ha'ar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. An old friend of fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Memit Nadill]], Sonam-Ha'ar was present at a [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] session in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], where Nadill and [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] warned of the impending return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Nadill and Teta's forebodings came true, and the Sith [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attacked]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with a seemingly unstoppable invasion force. Sonam-Ha'ar, Nadill, and a group of other Jedi fought in the battle, but despite their efforts, victory seemed unlikely. However, during the battle, a large portion of the Sith force suddenly disappeared, revealing that they were mere [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. Taking the opportunity to turn the tide of the battle, Sonam-Ha'ar led a charge into the heart of the Sith forces, and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] won the battle. ==Biography== {{Quote|What? The invasion alarms!''"
"''I did not arrive soon enough!|Sonam-Ha'ar and Memit Nadill, before the Battle of Coruscant|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]]}} Sonam-Ha'ar was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. During his time as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Sonam-Ha'ar became good friends with fellow Jedi Master [[Memit Nadill]]. In the year [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], who was in the service of [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], saw a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Alarmed, Odan-Urr, Empress Teta, and Nadill traveled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to reveal this omen to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Sonam-Ha'ar was one of several Jedi who attended the Senate session. Although most of the Jedi present dismissed Odan-Urr's warning, Sonam-Ha'ar refrained from comment. Odan-Urr's forebodings proved to be true when a large Sith [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] soon arrived in the Koros [[Star system/Legends|system]] and began an [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|assault]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]]. When the Sith began their attack, Memit Nadill sent an urgent message to Coruscant, fearing that the Sith would soon strike there and prompting Sonam-Ha'ar to meet Nadill at a landing pad on Coruscant with several other Jedi. Pledging that the Jedi would help in the coming battle, Sonam-Ha'ar began preparing for the coming fight; suddenly however, Coruscant's invasion alarms sounded. [[File:Untitled-TrueColor-03.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Sonam-Ha'ar during the invasion of Coruscant.]] A massive battle fleet, consisting of tens of thousands of [[Starship/Legends|ships]], appeared in the Coruscant skies. From the [[Warship/Legends|warships]] came Sith [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]], which brought the Sith invasion force to the planet. Facing a massive force of Sith [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors and [[Sith war behemoth]]s, Sonam-Ha'ar, Memit Nadill, and the other Jedi decided to make a stand on the steps of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. The Jedi leaped headlong into the fury of the [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|battle]] and fought with ferocity, but it seemed that the Sith would soon take the planet. Suddenly, however, the bulk of the Sith force simply disappeared; they were simple [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. Seeing an opportunity to turn the tide of the battle, Sonam-Ha'ar and the other Jedi charged at the Sith, annihilating the rest of the enemy forces and winning the Battle of Coruscant. Overcome by the joy of victory, Sonam-Ha'ar and the Jedi exclaimed that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] would never fall. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|The Republic will never fall!|Sonam-Ha'ar|}} Sonam-Ha'ar was a courageous and experienced Jedi Knight, one who showed no hesitation in standing and fighting against the Sith, even when the outcome of the battle appeared hopeless. He was a fierce supporter of the Republic and believed that the Republic would never fall. Ha'ar was good friends with Memit Nadill and stood by his side in the Battle of Coruscant. In battle, Sonam-Ha'ar wielded a blue-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Sonam-Ha'ar first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|''Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. He appeared briefly in the arc's fourth issue, and reappeared in ''Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} follow-up arc, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], where he had a more substantial role. In these appearances, Sonam-Ha'ar was drawn by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and preliminary black-and-white sketches of Sonam-Ha'ar appear in the back of ''Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} first issue.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] Sonam-Ha'ar was later depicted in a picture of the Battle of Coruscant drawn by [[Bill Hughes]] in [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], written by Anderson and [[Daniel Wallace]].[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] He also appeared in another illustration of the Battle of Coruscant in the reference book [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], drawn by [[Chris Trevas]].[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Sonam-Ha'ar}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] ==Sources== *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] {{Po}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Sonam-Ha'ar |pl=Sonam-Ha'ar }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Jedi Generals]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pre-Ruusan Jedi Masters]]
